reforms.description: The Italian government has introduced new measures to promote investment in depressed regions, including automatic incentives for investment and a fund for consolidating debts of small and medium enterprises. These initiatives aim to reduce structural unemployment and foster economic development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The incentives are designed to stimulate investment and economic activity in underdeveloped areas, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect to support investment in depressed regions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The measures represent a significant but narrower reform aimed at addressing regional disparities and supporting small enterprises.
reforms.source_quote: New measures for the development of depressed regions were introduced in August 1995, including automatic incentives for investment in depressed regions.

reforms.description: The Italian government established the Anti-Trust Commission in 1990 to enforce new anti-trust laws aimed at enhancing competition in the market, which is expected to foster innovation and efficiency in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing competition, the reforms are expected to stimulate innovation and improve market efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The Anti-Trust Commission has been operational since its establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Anti-Trust Commission represents a structural change in the regulatory framework aimed at promoting competition and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The anti-trust law on the 'protection of competition and the market' established the Anti-Trust Commission, an independent body empowered to enforce the new anti-trust law.
